title: Engine 181 Car Fire Attack Line
scene: Engine 181 is positioned on the street for a working car fire. A single 1¾" attack line is stretched from the engine to the vehicle. All labels are generated from this JSON using overlay coordinates.
studentQuestion: Engine 181 is operating at a car fire. The crew stretched 200′ of 1¾" hose with a fog nozzle flowing 150 GPM at 50 PSI. There is no elevation change and no appliance loss. What pump pressure should you set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss
FL = C × (GPM ÷ 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds
instructorExplanation: For this car fire, calculate friction loss in the 200′ 1¾" attack line, then add the 50 PSI fog nozzle pressure. There is no elevation change and no appliance loss.
explainMistake: Common mistakes include using the wrong C value, forgetting to convert 200′ into 2 hundred-foot sections, using 100 PSI nozzle pressure instead of 50 PSI, or adding appliance loss when none is listed.
